runs.text: This project presents an AI-based, language-agnostic automated code review and auto-fixing system that helps identify bugs, code smells, and inefficient coding practices. By combining static code analysis with AI-driven reasoning, the system provides clear explanations and suggested fixes, reducing manual review effort. The solution is deployed on a Linux-based cloud platform, improving scalability, code quality, and developer productivity.
Manual code review is time-consuming, inconsistent, and heavily dependent on human expertise.
Developers, especially beginners, struggle to identify syntax, logical, and coding standard issues, leading to poor code quality.
Existing tools lack intelligent automation for comprehensive code review, auto-fixing, and meaningful explanations, creating a need for an AI-based solution.
Hybrid AI-Based Code Analysis: Combines static analysis with AI reasoning for accurate bug detection and code review.
Language-Agnostic Design: Enables analysis across multiple programming languages without language-specific dependency.
Explainable Auto-Fixing System: Provides human-readable explanations along with automated code fix suggestions.
Large Language Models (LLMs) for code review
Cloud Platform: AWS / Google Cloud / Microsoft Azure
Hosting: Cloud VM / App Engine / ECS

The Code Reviewer & Auto-Fixer – Agent System successfully demonstrates how AI can enhance the software development process by automating code review and bug fixing. It provides fast, accurate, and consistent feedback, reduces dependency on human reviewers, and helps improve code quality while supporting learning for beginners. The modular agent-based design ensures scalability and maintainability, and cloud deployment allows accessibility from anywhere. Overall, the project showcases the practical application of AI in real-world coding environments, making software development more efficient, reliable, and developer-friendly.
